question: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $68,000 on April 20?

fragility_points: A move back into the 69k-70k area would materially weaken the Yes case.
Any Binance-specific market-data or execution anomaly around the target minute could matter because the contract is venue-specific and minute-specific.
key_assumptions: BTC does not suffer a sufficiently large downside move before noon ET on April 20 to push the Binance 1-minute close below 68000.
Binance trading and market data remain orderly enough that the settlement minute is representative.
main_logical_chain: Market implies 93.5% Yes from a current price of 0.935.
Current Binance spot around 74.3k puts BTC materially above the 68k threshold with six days left, so Yes is favored on the outside view.
But crypto volatility and single-minute settlement mechanics make 93.5% look somewhat rich.
Therefore the best base-rate estimate is high-Yes but below market confidence, around 87%.
payload.main_thesis: BTC is likely to finish above 68000 on the specified Binance noon ET minute, but the market is somewhat too confident given crypto's ability to move more than 8% in six days.
quote_anchors: This market will resolve to 'Yes' if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone has a final 'Close' price higher than the price specified.

strongest_disconfirmers: BTC has recently traded below 68000, and an 8%-9% drawdown in six days is plausible in crypto.

strongest_supports: Binance BTCUSDT was about 74306.58 at check time, roughly 6306 above the threshold.

unresolved_ambiguities: Minor UI-vs-API presentation ambiguity remains because the rules reference the Binance chart UI, though the underlying candle data should match.
